title: Memoization and stable identity

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Memoization and stable identity; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to Memoization and stable identity
sources.claim: memo, useMemo, and useCallback cache work or identities as performance hints when dependencies and comparison costs justify them.

canonicalExplanation.summary: memo, useMemo, and useCallback cache work or identities as performance hints when dependencies and comparison costs justify them.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A measured expensive filter uses useMemo with complete dependencies after simpler render computation proves slow. Your goal is to repair premature or ineffective memoization and demonstrate any retained optimization with evidence. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Routing and application context; Remote data and asynchronous UI; Measure before optimization; Dependencies and reactive values. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: memo, useMemo, and useCallback cache work or identities as performance hints when dependencies and comparison costs justify them. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Caches can be discarded, stale dependencies cause bugs, new object props defeat memo, and caching can cost more than recomputation. This draft therefore rejects: useMemo is required to make a computed value semantically correct and permanent.

includes: Use memo, useMemo or useCallback only when measured work or dependency identity requires it.
Repair premature or ineffective memoization and demonstrate any retained optimization with evidence.
excludes: Caches can be discarded, stale dependencies cause bugs, new object props defeat memo, and caching can cost more than recomputation.

outcomes: Repair premature or ineffective memoization and demonstrate any retained optimization with evidence.

examples.title: Memoization and stable identity: concrete case
examples.situation: A measured expensive filter uses useMemo with complete dependencies after simpler render computation proves slow.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A measured expensive filter uses useMemo with complete dependencies after simpler render computation proves slow.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Memoization is an optimization, not a correctness guarantee; first make logic pure and dependencies complete.
examples.result: The result follows because Memoization is an optimization, not a correctness guarantee; first make logic pure and dependencies complete.
